ks1.objective: Bowl or throw your ball to knock over the opponent's cones — collect each one you hit.
successCriteria: Bowls / throws towards a chosen cone

lks2.objective: Develop pitching and bowling accuracy — choose your stance and target to maximise hits.
successCriteria: Sets up the cones with thought (some easy, some hard targets)
Adjusts angle and power per cone
Aims at the cone, not just at the area

space.description: Two opposite zones 2–3m apart. Each child sets up their cones in their own zone.

steps: Give each child some cones (between 3–5 each).
They can set the cones up in any formation but must be 2–3m from their opponent.
Each child takes turns bowling/pitching their ball aiming to hit their opponent's cones.
If successful they take the cone they hit and add it to their own collection.
Game ends when one player has collected all the cones.
questions: What feedback can you give your opponent to help them?
How should you position your body to achieve success bowling?
What could you do to improve your score?

step.task: Children choose whether they bowl or throw
step.equipment: Give each child more or fewer cones
step.people: Play in pairs (two on each side, backing each other up)

errors.fix: Mark a no-block zone; reward clean raids
